question: Does the L-profile distinguish sampled, revised employment estimates (CES) from an administrative near-census of the same quantity (QCEW), on the multiplicative and encoding axes?

design.vintages: CES cross-sections of many series at three release vintages (preliminary / 1st revision / 2nd-or-later revision) via ALFRED real-time archive; administrative comparator = QCEW employment counts at matching reference period
design.unit: each vintage is a cross-section of many series values (state x supersector CES panel, matched across vintages); QCEW is area x industry employment counts
design.integerization: canonical: CES values are thousands with one decimal -> x10 integerization recorded as detected precision (encoding axis input), analyzed both as stored-integer (x10) and as detected-decimals feature; QCEW counts are native integers
design.matching: same series panel pulled at every vintage; series missing at any vintage dropped from all (matched cross-sections)

predictions.multiplicative_axis: keff and scale-clean residuals (dL1', dL2', dTail') ~ null (keff in [0.9, 1.1]; residual 90% CIs include 0) across ALL four vintages - employment figures are additive tallies; confirms the recording boundary
predictions.encoding_axis: sampled estimates (initial and revisions) carry a rounding/precision signature (rounding_mass elevated, coarser detected precision) that the administrative exact count does not; signature strongest at initial, weakest at administrative
predictions.revision_drift: encoding coordinates move monotonically initial -> 1st rev -> 2nd rev -> administrative (series converges toward the administrative encoding as it is completed)
predictions.vs_cP: if the encoding axis separates sample from administrative where cP was null on count-vs-estimate, that is L-profile utility on the exact contrast cP could not read; if BOTH axes are null, that is the strongest confirmation that employment counts are multiplicatively and encoding-generic

decision_rules.boundary_confirmed: multiplicative axis null at every vintage per the CI rule above
decision_rules.provenance_signal: encoding contrast (initial CES vs QCEW) bootstrap 90% CI on rounding_mass difference excludes 0 AND direction matches prediction
decision_rules.drift: monotone ordering of the primary encoding coordinate across the four vintages (Kendall tau of coordinate vs vintage rank, all pairwise steps in predicted direction)

instruments: same corpus-wide instruments, not re-tuned: baseline.csv digit-conditioned null, baseline_derounded_v2 scale-clean coordinates, rounding_mass/decimals/resonance encoding coordinates, family-level bootstrap CIs
acquisition_plan: CES state x supersector (not seasonally adjusted) via ALFRED alfredgraph csv vintage interface (no key) or FRED API if key available; QCEW via open BLS data slices (data.bls.gov/cew/data/api); every deviation logged
hygiene: predictions frozen before any fetch; cross-sections matched across vintages; encoding coordinates identical to corpus-wide definitions; seeds and workers recorded; figures PNG dpi 200
